to compute the sum of absolute differences for each element in the sorted array, we need to calculate the absolute differences between the current element and all other elements. for elements at index i, if i is a multiple of 3, we calculate the square of the difference instead of the absolute difference.

for the first part, we calculate the sum of absolute differences for each element. this involves iterating through the array and for each element, calculating the absolute difference with all other elements. for elements at index i, if i is a multiple of 3, we calculate the square of the difference instead.

*** 

for the second part, we calculate the sum of squared differences for elements at index i that are multiples of 3. this involves iterating through the array and for each element at index i that is a multiple of 3, calculating the squared difference with all other elements. the result array is built by combining the results of the first part and the second part.
